channel.title: Computer Vision on Slow Data
channel.description: Recent content in Computer Vision on Slow Data

item.title: Poisson surface reconstruction
item.description: A laser scan of a 3D object hands you a set of point and a set of vectors: samples sitting on the object’s surface and the normals at each of these points. That’s awkward, because almost everything you’d want to do with the object (render it, 3D-print it, measure it, drop it in a physics engine) needs an actual surface, not a point cloud. So the question is: given only these samples, how do we recover the surface they came from?
